summary: MEDIUM ux-next-command-shell-unsafe-placeholder: followup-decision-artifact and design-diagrams-final-spec emit next_command strings that read as directly-runnable vibepro commands but contain unquoted <...> placeholders, which bash parses as redirection — pasting them verbatim produces a syntax error (reviewer reproduced via bash -c). Inconsistent with generic-token-clause-binding which shellQuotes its fallback and with architecture-reason-frontmatter which uses an explicit comment-prefixed non-command convention. Fix by quoting the placeholder segments or adopting the comment-prefix convention. Other UX surfaces (machine-readable results, planned/failed state distinction, artifacts[] traceability) are solid.

inspection.summary: Read all 6 recipes and the render section, checked spec scenarios and test assertions (regex-only, no shell-validity check), ran the e2e suite (8/8), and executed both offending next_command strings via bash -c reproducing the syntax errors.
inspection.evidence: bash -c reproduction of both next_command strings (syntax error); node --test e2e spec (8/8, run by reviewer)

judgment_delta: initial: 4/6 recipes use shellQuote consistently suggesting uniform copy-paste safety -> final: needs_changes, because actually executing the 2 offending strings proved they fail as shell input rather than serving as copy-paste-ready commands
